title: PoisonedRAG: Knowledge Corruption Attacks to Retrieval-Augmented Generation of Large Language Models
why_relevant: Foundational attack paper: injects 5 poisoned texts to achieve >90% attack success on NQ/HotpotQA/MS-MARCO; defines the knowledge corruption threat model for RAG.

title: Not What You've Signed Up For: Compromising Real-World LLM-Integrated Applications with Indirect Prompt Injection
why_relevant: Seminal paper on indirect prompt injection via retrieved content; shows RAG attack surface in real applications; 1225 citations makes it the most cited paper in this space.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
why_relevant: Foundational RAG paper (Lewis et al. 2020, NeurIPS); establishes the parametric + non-parametric memory paradigm that all security work builds on.

title: RAGTruth: A Hallucination Corpus for Developing Trustworthy Retrieval-Augmented Language Models
why_relevant: ACL 2024 benchmark: 18,000 naturally-generated RAG responses with word-level hallucination annotations; key resource for faithfulness/trustworthiness research.

title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
why_relevant: ICLR 2024 best paper; introduces critique-and-retrieve loop for faithfulness; directly relevant to hallucination/ethics dimension of RAG reliability.

title: The Attacker Moves Second: Stronger Adaptive Attacks Bypass Defenses Against LLM Jailbreaks and Prompt Injections
why_relevant: Shows adaptive attacks consistently bypass prompt injection and jailbreak defenses; directly relevant to evaluating defense robustness in RAG pipelines.

title: Traceback of Poisoning Attacks to Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: WWW 2025: first forensic traceback method for poisoned texts in RAG; important defense/accountability mechanism.

title: BadRAG: Identifying Vulnerabilities in Retrieval Augmented Generation of Large Language Models
why_relevant: Introduces retrieval backdoor via MCOP; 10 adversarial passages achieves 98.2% retrieval success; key attack taxonomy paper.

title: TrojanRAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Can Be Backdoor Driver in Large Language Models
why_relevant: Joint backdoor attack using knowledge graph for fine-grained retrieval manipulation; shows RAG as universal attack driver.

title: Is My Data in Your Retrieval Database? Membership Inference Attacks Against Retrieval Augmented Generation
why_relevant: ICISSP 2025: first systematic MIA against RAG knowledge bases; defines S²MIA, MBA, IA attack variants; key privacy threat characterization.

title: Towards Trustworthy Retrieval Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
why_relevant: Comprehensive 2025 survey covering RAG trustworthiness: reliability, privacy, safety, fairness, explainability, accountability.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey of Architectures, Enhancements, and Robustness Frontiers
why_relevant: 2025 comprehensive survey covering RAG robustness, adversarial scenarios, noise filtering; provides systematic overview of the field.

title: Riddle Me This! Stealthy Membership Inference for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
venue: Conference on Computer and Communications Security (CCS)
why_relevant: CCS 2025: stealthy MIA using riddle-style queries; demonstrates practical privacy threat against production RAG systems.

title: Mitigating Hallucination in Large Language Models (LLMs): An Application-Oriented Survey on RAG, Reasoning, and Agentic Systems
why_relevant: Application-oriented survey on hallucination mitigation in RAG; covers knowledge-based vs. logic-based hallucination taxonomy.

title: Trustworthiness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems: A Survey
why_relevant: Proposes unified trustworthiness framework for RAG across six dimensions: factuality, robustness, fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy.

title: No Free Lunch: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Undermines Fairness in LLMs, Even for Vigilant Users
why_relevant: EMNLP 2024: shows RAG degrades fairness even with fully censored corpora; 20% unfair samples sufficient to elicit biased output; key fairness finding.

title: Safeguarding Privacy of Retrieval Data against Membership Inference Attacks: Is This Query Too Close to Home?
why_relevant: EMNLP 2025: query-similarity-based MIA detection; practical privacy safeguard for RAG deployment.

title: Securing Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Taxonomy of Attacks, Defenses, and Future Directions
why_relevant: Most recent (April 2026) taxonomy paper; classifies RAG attacks by trust boundary and attack surface; key reference for systematic threat modeling.

title: Practical Poisoning Attacks against Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: Demonstrates practical, low-resource poisoning attacks; bridges gap between theoretical attack models and real-world deployability.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on LLM Defenses Against Prompt Injection and Jailbreaking
why_relevant: 2026 systematic review synthesizing 128 studies on prompt injection defenses; covers detection, prompt augmentation, training-based and system-level defenses.

title: A Survey on Hallucination in Large Language Models: Principles, Taxonomy, Challenges and Open Questions
why_relevant: Seminal hallucination survey; high-cited foundational reference for faithfulness/trustworthiness dimension of RAG ethics; provides terminology and taxonomy used across the field.

title: Data Extraction Attacks in Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Backdoors
why_relevant: Backdoor-based PII extraction from RAG corpus; 94.1% verbatim extraction rate with 5% poisoned fine-tuning data.

title: Backdoored Retrievers for Prompt Injection Attacks on Retrieval Augmented Generation of Large Language Models
why_relevant: Backdoors the dense retriever fine-tuning process to achieve persistent prompt injection via retrieval; novel attack vector distinct from corpus poisoning.

title: RAG Security and Privacy: Formalizing the Threat Model and Attack Surface
why_relevant: ICDMW 2025: formal threat model for RAG; identifies four primary security surfaces and three trust boundaries.

title: Defending Against Knowledge Poisoning Attacks During Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: Defense at inference time against knowledge poisoning; does not require re-indexing the corpus.

title: Privacy-Aware Decoding: Mitigating Privacy Leakage of Large Language Models in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: First decoding-time defense for RAG privacy; token-level noise injection with formal RDP accounting.

title: Does RAG Introduce Unfairness in LLMs? Evaluating Fairness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
why_relevant: Empirical evaluation framework for RAG fairness; measures disparities across demographic attributes in retrieval and generation stages.

title: Differentially Private Synthetic Text Generation for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: DP synthetic data as privacy-preserving RAG corpus; explores trade-off between privacy budget and generation quality.

title: Privacy-Preserving Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Differential Privacy
why_relevant: DPVoteRAG algorithm spending privacy budget selectively on sensitive tokens; formal DP guarantee for RAG.

title: Towards More Robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Evaluating RAG Under Adversarial Poisoning Attacks
why_relevant: Systematic evaluation of RAG robustness to adversarial poisoning; proposes defense heuristics.

title: RAGPart & RAGMask: Retrieval-Stage Defenses Against Corpus Poisoning in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: RAGPart (document partitioning) and RAGMask (token masking) as retrieval-stage defenses; reduces attack success while preserving utility.

title: Exploring the Security Threats of Retriever Backdoors in Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation
why_relevant: Extends backdoor threat to code generation RAG; shows software supply chain risks.

title: Semantic Chameleon: Corpus-Dependent Poisoning Attacks and Defenses in RAG Systems
why_relevant: Novel corpus-dependent poisoning that remains dormant for benign queries; directly connects to OWASP LLM Top 10 vector embedding risk.

title: PIDP-Attack: Combining Prompt Injection with Database Poisoning Attacks on Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
why_relevant: Compound attack synergizing query-path prompt injection with database poisoning; no prior knowledge of user query needed.

title: Addressing Corpus Knowledge Poisoning Attacks on RAG Using Sparse Attention
why_relevant: SDAG defense: block-sparse attention disabling cross-document attention to prevent poisoned context propagation.

title: Towards Secure Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Review of Threats, Defenses, and Future Directions
why_relevant: 2026 comprehensive review of RAG security threats and defenses; good synthesis of the current state of the field.

title: Benchmarking Knowledge-Extraction Attack and Defense on Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: Unified benchmark for knowledge-extraction attacks and defenses on RAG; covers RAGThief, IKEA, RAGCRAWLER under one framework.

title: Silent Leaks: Implicit Knowledge Extraction Attack on RAG Systems through Benign Queries
why_relevant: IKEA attack: 91% extraction efficiency using benign queries and anchor-concept exploration; no jailbreaking needed.

title: The Other Side of the Coin: Exploring Fairness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: Metamorphic testing for RAG fairness; small perturbations reveal bias in retrieval stage.

title: Connect the Dots: Knowledge Graph-Guided Crawler Attack on Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems
why_relevant: RAGCrawler: knowledge-graph-guided systematic extraction achieving 76.6% coverage; most effective knowledge-base stealing attack.

title: RAG Safety: Exploring Knowledge Poisoning Attacks to Retrieval-Augmented Generation
why_relevant: Systematic exploration of knowledge poisoning attack space; safety-focused perspective on RAG vulnerabilities.

title: Fairness Testing in Retrieval-Augmented Generation: How Small Perturbations Reveal Bias in Small Language Models
why_relevant: Metamorphic testing reveals fairness failures in RAG with small language models; broadens beyond GPT-scale systems.

title: Feedback-Guided Extraction of Knowledge Base from Retrieval-Augmented LLM Applications
why_relevant: Systematic knowledge-base extraction via feedback-guided iterative queries; early work in the extraction attack family.
